Title: On Algeria, Macron has a goal for his last year in office: Reconciliation
Subhead: The French president, who began his first term aiming to ease historical tensions, now faces the most severe crisis in bilateral relations since Algeria's independence in 1962.

Title: Trump halts Hormuz escort operation after one day
Kyiv's unilateral ceasefire already broken by Russian strikes
'With the withdrawal of American troops from Germany, Berlin is facing a historic dilemma'
Subhead: Donald Trump's announcement of the withdrawal of US troops is forcing Berlin to confront the long-standing taboo of its own defense, explains the specialist in Franco-German relations Paul Maurice for Le Monde.

Title: Sweden's right is now normalizing the far-right party, born from the fascist movement
Subhead: With legislative elections scheduled for September 13 approaching, the Conservatives, Liberals and Christian Democrats have signaled their willingness to govern with the Sweden Democrats, the far-right party that enabled them to return to power in 2022.

Title: Chinese artist Gao Zhen and his family stuck in repressive legal limbo over his Mao sculptures
Subhead: Authorities are accusing the visual artist, a US resident, over works created in the 2000s that explicitly criticized Chairman Mao's regime. His wife holds out faint hope that Donald Trump's upcoming visit to Beijing, on May 14 and 15, can help her husband's case.

Title: German Chancellor Merz hits a new low one year after taking office
Subhead: At the helm of a coalition plagued by disagreements and criticized for his verbal gaffes, the German head of government is the most unpopular chancellor since the founding of the Federal Republic.

Subhead: Hantavirus is responsible for the death of at least one of the three passengers who died this past month. While another three people on board are showing symptoms of acute respiratory distress, the luxury cruise ship, which Cape Verdean authorities have banned from docking, could head for the Canary Islands.

Title: Hundreds of flight cancellations leave passengers frustrated and stranded
Subhead: The war in the Middle East has caused the price of jet fuel to double in just a few weeks. Many airlines, especially low-cost carriers, have canceled flights with low occupancy or low profitability. Passenger rights are protected, but within certain limits.

Title: Putin's waning popularity worries Kremlin five months ahead of Russian parliamentary elections
Subhead: Internet shutdowns have hastened the decline in public trust in the president, amid economic hardships that have affected citizens' daily lives and the war in Ukraine that has only kept dragging on.
